page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.7 GHz. The server features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations ranking in the top 10 percent. Database operations, including Redis SET and PassMark database tests, also achieve top-tier results. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

This server is designed for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads such as large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and software compilation. While prompt processing for large language models registers strong to average performance, text generation speeds are a notable tradeoff, ranking as weak for small models and average for medium to large models. The high density of physical cores and massive DDR5 memory capacity offer high resource efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise applications, though workloads reliant on single-core speed or GPU acceleration will not benefit from this architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the 2.7 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with outstanding results in database operations and multi-threaded decompression. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds represent clear tradeoffs, scoring in the average to weak ranges. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive, parallelized workloads such as large-scale databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, where its massive physical core count and high-density memory can be fully leveraged.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARMv9 platform featuring 192 physical cores, massive DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.7 GHz
1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8 GB per physical core
11400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store capacity
Top-tier multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Moderate single-core performance and limited LLM text generation speeds
Optimized for large-scale databases, parallel compilation, and high-throughput web serving
